items.title: Primitive values and typeof: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications misconception? Strings are mutable arrays because bracket access returns characters.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. String primitives are immutable; operations return new values rather than changing characters in place.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. String primitives are immutable; operations return new values rather than changing characters in place.
